rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. MOATS ET AL VS. MICHAEL TSANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joint Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To Amend Answer Of Defendants Nrt West, Inc. Dba Coldwell Banker Global Luxury And Naomi Glass
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 6, 2023, line 1. Defendants NRT West and Naomi Glass' Motion for Leave to Amend Answer is GRANTED. Amended Answer shall be filed and served forthwith. No opposition filed. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. PABLO E. RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Naifeh Azar'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Issues And Or Causes Of Action For Ejectment From Other Causes And Issues
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 6, 2023, line 2. Plaintiff's Motion to Bifurcate is DENIED. Moving party failed to establish that bifurcation of the cause of action for ejectment for trial will promote convenience of witness and/or economy and effacing of handling the litigation. To the extent the bifurcation is sought to promote "the ends of justice," moving party failed to establish that bifurcation would result in the cause of action for ejectment being tried sooner that the rest of the action. Late-filed opposition is stricken. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG P. RAMSDELL VS. 88 KING OWNERS ASSOCIATIONS, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 6, 2023, line 3. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a viable cause of action for declaratory relief including standing to challenge the CC&R; standing to bring an action for violation of 2 C.C.R. 12005 et seq., and an actual controversy. Plaintiff must allege facts, not conclusions to support each element of each cause of action. The Court does not reach any other arguments raised in the demurrer at this time. These arguments may be brought up later if necessary and proper. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAYNI KOSOFF ET AL VS. CARSTEN PHILIP HOMANN AKA HOMANN CARSTEN PHILIP ET AL

rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 6, 2023, line 5. Plaintiff's Motion to Vacate Dismissal is GRANTED.
